treatments.label: Ankle dorsiflexion eccentrically in sitting with/without electrical stimulation
Swing phase ensuring heel strike maintaining dorsiflexion then lowering forefoot to the floor
Walking emphasising walking with heel strike at Initial Contact
Use of footprints/small circles for heel contact overground or markers on treadmill
Soleus: Sitting - pushing heel off the ground (make sure not compensating with hip flexors to lift their thigh)
Gastrocs in standing on a reverse wedge with back against wall and lower heels down to floor to fatigue

Back against wall, move body forwards away 
from wall by dorsiflexing at ankles – increase body weight on affected leg (eg scales to monitor)

Step intact leg forward systematically increasing step lengths (again can use back against wall to promote forward translation)–make sure that weight moves forward on affected leg while stepping (can use scales under forefoot only to give feedback on weight translation onto forefoot)
Walking with visual/auditory cues to increase step length
Walking on treadmill with visual/auditory cues
Plantarflex in lying against resistance (wall, end of bed, tilt-table preferably with feedback eg scales, pressure monitor)

Standing with back to the wall, affected leg on a dorsiflexion wedge and intact leg on a block. Person instructed to raise heel rapidly. Remember to ask person to keep back on wall and avoid unloading affected leg
Practise plantarflexing in standing, : verbal cueing to emphasise eccentric control and to switch rapidly between eccentric and concentric activity.
Step intact foot forward, use scales under affected fore-foot (to make sure weight is translated into forefoot and pushing into plantar flexion)
Step intact leg forward and up onto block to ensure need to plantarflex in SLS on affected leg
Walking training using cueing strategies to increase step length
Increased speed of walking to demand increased plantarflexor activity for push off
Verbal cues regarding the timing of push-off (e.g. the affected heel should be off the ground before the other foot touches the ground
Person standing with back to wall with intact leg on block. Person instructed to flex affected knee and extend back to wall
Stepping affected leg forward and flexing knee to target
Step off block with affected leg to increase amount of ground reaction force on landing, flex knee to target after landing

Person standing with back to wall with intact leg on block. Person instructed to flex affected knee and extend back to wall, adding wedge to put ankle into PF in order to make it more difficult for knee to maintain extension
Stepping intact leg forward (or onto a block) while maintaining knee extension on affected leg
Walking practice with verbal cues to keep affected knee in flexion (avoid hyperextension)
Stepping intact leg forward, ensuring that hip on affected leg moves into extension, use target if necessary

Moving intact leg from behind affected leg and step wih intact leg forward, ensuring that hip on affected leg moves into extension, use target if necessary, this forces hip extensors to work from a flexed position
Stepping intact leg forward and up onto a block this forces the hip extensors to work harder (as well as the plantarflexors)
Walking with auditory cues to increase cadence
Stepping intact leg in different directions e.g. out vs. across midline to increase abduction and adduction
Person standing with back to wall and stepping intact leg on/off block with target for amount of lateral pelvic shift over stance leg
Person standing with back to wall with narrow BOS, step with intact leg on/off block with target for amount of lateral pelvic shift over stance leg
Walking with visual cues for base of support (BOS)
ROM for gastrocs, / AFO whilst walking

kinematic_deviations.label: Decreased ankle dorsiflexion at initial contact (heel strike)
Decreased ankle dorsiflexion from foot flat to heel off
Decreased ankle plantarflexion from heel off to toe off
Decreased knee flexion at initial contact to foot flat
Decreased knee extension at foot flat to mid stance or knee  hyperextension
Decreased hip extension from initial contact to late stance phase
Decreased or increased hip adduction at initial contact to mid stance
Increased or decreased hip abduction from mid stance to toe-off

impairments.impairment: Decreased eccentric strength in ankle dorsiflexors
impairments.testing: MMT ankle dorsiflexors eccentrically, sitting, knee flexed – extended

impairments.impairment: Ability to maintain dorsiflexion as knee is extended
impairments.testing: Test ability to dorsiflex, dorsiflex slowly and maintain dorsiflexion as knee moves into extension

impairments.impairment: Decreased sensation
impairments.testing: proprioception test / sensation test

impairments.impairment: Decreased passive ankle's dorsiflexion
impairments.testing: passive movement test/ Tardieu scale

impairments.impairment: Decreased eccentric strength in ankle plantarflexors (Soleus and gastrocs)
impairments.testing: MMT ankle plantarflexors eccentrically: Soleus: sitting, Gastrocnemius: with knee extended sitting/standing

impairments.impairment: Decreased ability to coordinate eccentric ankle plantarflexors, with other Lower Limb extensors and hip abductors through stance phase
impairments.testing: Test ability to move body forward over foot in single leg stance (SLS)

impairments.impairment: Decreased ability to prevent knee hyperextension in mid stance as LL moves over foot
impairments.testing: Test ability to move body forward over foot in single leg stance (SLS)

impairments.impairment: Decreased concentric  strength ankle plantarflexors: Soleus, Gastrocnemius
impairments.testing: MMT Soleus: sitting, Gastrocnemius: knee in extension – sitting, standing, SLS

impairments.impairment: Decreased ability to rapidly plantarflex ankle in SLS
impairments.testing: Test ability to rapidly plantarflex ankle in SLS

impairments.impairment: Decreased strength knee flexors concentrically prior to and at heel strike
impairments.testing: MMT – Knee flexors concentrically with knee in almost full extension (consider closed vs open chain)

impairments.impairment: Decreased ability to coordinate knee flexors with ankle plantarflexors eccentrically at heel strike
impairments.testing: Ability to control knee flexion/extension in almost full knee extension

impairments.impairment: Decreased passive knee flexion (unlikely unless very severe)
impairments.testing: Passive ROM to check for mm length changes/knee joint stiffness

impairments.impairment: Decreased concentric strength knee extensors
impairments.testing: MMT – check inner range strength of knee extensors

impairments.impairment: Decreased ability to coordinate knee extensors, knee flexors and ankle plantarflexors
impairments.testing: Test ability to extend knee in SLS

impairments.impairment: Decreased strength hip extensors working concentrically at initial contact
impairments.testing: MMT of: Hip extensors concentically

impairments.impairment: Decreased strength hip flexors working eccentrically during the second half of stance
impairments.testing: MMT Hip flexors eccentrically

impairments.impairment: Decreased ability to coordinate all LL extensors and hip abductors
impairments.testing: Test ability to maintain SLS with knee and hip extended

impairments.impairment: Decreased passive hip extension
impairments.testing: Muscle length hip flexors/ hip joint stiffness

impairments.impairment: Decreased strength hip abductors
impairments.testing: MMT of hip abductors side lying/supine

impairments.impairment: Decreased ability to coordinate eccentric hip abductors with leg extensors
impairments.testing: Test ability to stand in SLS and control pelvic shift over stance leg

impairments.impairment: Decreased ability to change from eccentric to concentric hip abductor activity
impairments.testing: Test ability to stand in SLS and control pelvic shift over stance leg
